$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `chat_documents` repo (Phase-2). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness chat_documents tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: a plain (non-Taggable) AbstractBaseRepository with an
all-text-plus-one-boolean shape and no number / JSON / BLOB columns. Two
required TEXT strings (chatId, filePath), an enum TEXT column (scope:
(mountPoint, displayTitle -> NULL), and one boolean (isActive -> INTEGER 0/1).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / image_profiles fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday
pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New active document, scope 'general', mountPoint + displayTitle set.
New inactive document, scope 'project', both nullable columns null.
Update seed row 2: flip isActive false->true and set displayTitle. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row 1: change scope document_store->general and set a new mountPoint. updatedAt pinned.
